description_long: This object is a twin-engine, propeller-driven airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic WWII-era design. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, with two engines housed in nacelles on each wing. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and features a multi-panel canopy with visible framing, suggesting an enclosed, multi-seat configuration. The tail section consists of a conventional single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a standard tail assembly. The landing gear is not visible, implying it may be retractable or not modeled in this representation. The exterior is finished in a muted, military-style gray-green color scheme with subtle panel lines and minimal markings.
description: A twin-engine, propeller-driven airplane with a streamlined fuselage, mid-mounted straight wings, and a classic military gray-green finish. Features an enclosed, multi-panel cockpit and a conventional tail assembly.
description_view_0: Front view shows the twin propellers, the streamlined nose, and the symmetrical mid-mounted wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated fuselage, cockpit canopy, and the engine nacelle on the wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the single vertical stabilizer, horizontal stabilizers, and the tail cone.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the other engine nacelle and the full length of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or not modeled.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy, likely multi-seat.
